channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: A License Is Not a Teacher
item.description: Ask why so many Indian classrooms struggle, and the answers arrive in the language of audit: Too few trained teachers, too many vacancies, weak colleges of education and low accountability. Each of these is real, and each matters. Yet none of them explains a quiet confession a veteran teacher made to me, years into her […]

item.title: The Cost of Excluding Women Combatants From Demobilization Is High
item.description: Disarmament, demobilization and reintegration, or DDR, is quietly returning to the center of international policy as the world grapples with how to resolve many large-scale conflicts and to support lasting peace. From Ukraine and Gaza to Sudan and Lebanon, questions about who is a participant in conflict, who receives support and how reintegration unfolds will ... Read more

item.title: ‘This is a fire’: DRC Ebola outbreak is fastest-growing ever, warns WHO
item.description: Infections of the Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have reached record highs and a majority of new cases are coming from “unknown chains of transmission”, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Tuesday.

item.title: People continue to stand by refugees, Gen Z shows most empathy
item.description: Protection for refugees has been enshrined in international law for more than seven decades, but how much support is there today for those fleeing conflict and persecution?

item.title: ‘Hunted’ by drones: UN reports worst month for Ukraine’s civilians since 2022
item.description: Civilian casualties in Ukraine soared during the first half of 2026 amid escalating Russian attacks and intensifying use of deadly weapons, the UN human rights monitoring mission in the country, HRMMU, said on Tuesday.

item.title: ‘Cycle of escalation must end’: UN condemns deadly Strait of Hormuz attacks
item.description: The UN maritime agency, IMO, condemned overnight attacks on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz that killed at least two seafarers as fresh strikes were reported early Tuesday in the escalating US-Iran war.

item.title: Independent expert urges UK leaders to uphold Supreme Court ruling on women's rights
item.description: A UN independent human rights expert has urged political leaders in the United Kingdom to support implementation of a Supreme Court ruling on the legal definition of sex, warning that opposition to updated equality guidance risks weakening protections for women and girls.

item.title: World Cup football’s other goal: Helping young people talk about mental health
item.description: When Dr. Sahira Al Nahari founded Shifā Art, a Saudi Arabia-based organization using art to create conversations around mental health, she noticed that men attending her therapeutic art workshops often felt uncomfortable opening up about their feelings.

item.title: World News in Brief: Gaza aid challenges persist, renewed push for clean energy, Sudan cholera update
item.description: Humanitarians in the Gaza Strip continue to face significant challenges in reaching communities located near the so-called “Yellow Line” in northern Rafah, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Tuesday.

item.title: West Africa and the Sahel: Terrorism is changing its face
item.description: Jihadist groups are no longer content with launching attacks in West Africa and the Sahel: they administer territories, control trade routes, exploit new technologies and are gradually pushing their influence all the way to the Gulf of Guinea.

item.title: Landmark Ruling Could Redefine Divorced Women’s Property Rights in Pakistan
item.description: A landmark Islamabad High Court ruling that recognised marriage as an economic partnership and awarded a divorced woman an equal share of assets acquired during marriage has triggered a legal and religious backlash, with Pakistan’s law ministry challenging the judgment before the Federal Shariat Court, a constitutional court empowered to determine whether laws and judicial […]

item.title: Western Imperialist Unity Split by Rival Priorities
item.description: Trump insists the West must unite on his terms against the Rest, particularly China and Iran. Europe, however, wants greater Trump support for Ukraine’s Zelensky regime to replace Putin’s leadership of Russia. Europe v China? In June 2026, European officials accused China of training Russian military personnel to fight in Ukraine. After Secretary of State […]

item.title: Arab Regions Remain the Most Underrepresented in the Global Trade System
item.description: Despite the importance of international trade as an engine for economic growth and development, only fourteen of the twenty-two Arab states are members of the World Trade Organization (WTO). The remaining Arab states risk missing out on opportunities for greater integration into the global economy and the multilateral trading system facilitated by the WTO. A […]

item.title: The Tale of Two Countries: Elite Stake and Development
item.description: Philippines was the most advanced Southeast Asian country with the highest per capita GDP until about the early 1960s. Its per capita GDP in purchasing power parity terms were about the same as South Korea’s and above that of Thailand in the early 1970s.The Nobel Laureate economist, Gunnar Myrdal, did not have much hope for […]
